title: libpod: warn if custom value is overriden by libpod
body:
* Libpod will override certain values if they are already set in backend
  database irrespective of the custom value set by the user.

* Add notes to man pages about these cli config flags

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]
Existing tests must pass, this is not a new feature or a bug fix.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR

string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
 llibpod: warn if custom value is overriden by libpod
```

Closes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/18430

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [flouthoc] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Yes this is something which should be discussed hence I created an issue, and also wondering what is the expectation of `--volumepath` since currently it does not looks functional.

>  since currently it does not looks functional.

It is just like the other --tmpdir/--runroot options, you need to set it on the first podman run in order to get that path into the db, afterwards the db will overwrite it every time. Run `podman system reset` then the first podman command after that with `--volumepath` and you will see that it works

> > since currently it does not looks functional.
> 
> It is just like the other --tmpdir/--runroot options, you need to set it on the first podman run in order to get that path into the db, afterwards the db will overwrite it every time. Run `podman system reset` then the first podman command after that with `--volumepath` and you will see that it works

@Luap99 Yes this is my concern that if db is already initialized the flags becomes `no-op`, I wonder if this is the intention behind these flags ( if that is expected behavior maybe it needs to be documented ) , if not then maybe this needs to be fixed. I am not sure what was the original intention behind flags hence created an issue.

This was, indeed, deliberate. We do not allow changing key paths - volume path included - because it breaks things when Podman A and Podman B have different values for them. Suddenly, two containers mounting the same volume are actually mounting different things. As for the concern about flags being no-ops after init, that's mostly intentional - the flags are only there for first-run, really, we don't expect these to be used regularly.

I don't see a reason to merge given this, unless we have a really conclusive reason to do so.
@mheon Ok seems fair, let me update the PR to amend the docs because its not very obvious for a cli flag to work only in certain conditions ( atleast docs should say so)

Perhaps a better phrasing would be "This option is intended to be used on the first run of Podman, after which its value will be set as the default until `podman system reset` is run. The default cannot be overridden without `podman system reset`"
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
@flouthoc, friendly ping.  Still interested in this PR?
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
Let's close this PR on the next stale.  I am going through inactive PRs and think we should close the ones abandoned ones.  That'll give others space to kick things over the finish line.
@vrothberg Agreed, closing this for now, I'll re-open when i'll pick this again.
